notable_pick_trades: Acquired extra 4th (No. 132 from SEA) and extra 5th (No. 170 from SEA) via prior trades. Hold compensatory 4th (No. 136). Possible additional pick(s) if Derek Carr un-retires and is traded (Day 3 range).
roster_context: The Saints went 6-11 in 2025 under first-year HC Kellen Moore but finished strong at 5-3 in the final 8 games once 2nd-round rookie QB Tyler Shough took over. Shough showed franchise-QB promise (68% completion rate on intermediate throws, 2nd in OROY voting). Chris Olave had a career year (100 rec, 1,163 yds, 9 TDs). The defense improved dramatically under DC Brandon Staley, finishing as one of the NFL's best units in the second half. Chase Young had a breakout year (10 sacks in 12 games). However, the roster has significant holes at WR2, RB, IOL, and EDGE depth, plus key free agents (Alontae Taylor, Demario Davis, Cameron Jordan, Taysom Hill) are all pending. Cap situation is much improved after Derek Carr's retirement but still requires maneuvering around ~$65M in dead money. The Saints are in position to be aggressive this offseason for the first time in years.

needs.explanation: Kool-Aid McKinstry took a step forward as the CB1 in 2025 (3 INTs, 17 PBUs, started all 17 games) but Bleacher Report noted his play hasn't quite been CB1-caliber consistently. Alontae Taylor (UFA) is expected to test the market and command $18M+/year — the Saints primarily used him in the slot and he appears likely to leave. If Taylor departs, the nickel/slot CB role is wide open. Quincy Riley (2025 4th-rounder) showed promise (1 INT, 10 PBUs) and could start opposite McKinstry, but there's no proven slot replacement for Taylor.
needs.current_starter: Kool-Aid McKinstry (CB1, under contract), Quincy Riley (CB2, under contract), Alontae Taylor (slot/nickel, UFA — likely departing)
needs.depth: Rezjohn Wright (ERFA), Beanie Bishop (ERFA), Michael Davis (UFA) — thin if Taylor leaves

needs.explanation: Chase Young (26) emerged as a cornerstone with 10 sacks in 12 games, but beyond him the edge group is thin and aging. Cameron Jordan (36, UFA) had 10.5 sacks but is a free agent at age 37 — re-signing him on a short deal helps but doesn't solve the long-term need. Carl Granderson had a down season. Chris Rumph and Jonah Williams are both UFAs. The Saints need a young pass rusher to develop opposite Young. EDGE was the 3rd most frequently mocked position to NO.
needs.current_starter: Chase Young (DE1, under contract through 2027), Cameron Jordan (UFA — 37 years old, wants to return)
needs.depth: Carl Granderson, Fadil Diggs (2025 7th-round pick) — need young rotational pass rusher

needs.explanation: Chris Olave is the only proven weapon in the passing game. No other WR on the roster stands out as anything more than an ancillary target. Devaughn Vele showed flashes (25 rec, 293 yds in 13 games) but was inconsistent. Trey Palmer and Samori Toure are depth pieces. Rashid Shaheed was traded to Seattle mid-season. Brandin Cooks was released. The Saints need a true WR2 to pair with Olave and support Shough in Year 2. WR is the most frequently mocked position to the Saints at No. 8 overall across 179 pre-Combine mock drafts.
needs.current_starter: Chris Olave (WR1, under contract on 5th-year option), Devaughn Vele (WR2 by default)
needs.depth: Trey Palmer, Samori Toure (RFA), Ronnie Bell (RFA), Kevin Austin (ERFA) — very thin beyond Olave
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 (No. 8 overall) — Carnell Tate, Emeka Egbuka, or other top WR prospect

needs.explanation: Ridgeway signing (2yr/$6.2M, depth) adds rotation depth but doesn't address a starter-level IDL need. Carl Granderson and Chase Young are the primary starters. IDL remains a tier 3 mid-to-late round depth target.

Demario Davis (37, UFA) is the heart of the defense with 143 tackles in 2025, but he's a free agent at age 37. The Saints want to re-sign him but it's not guaranteed. Pete Werner is solid but has limitations against the run on the edge. Danny Stutsman (2025 4th-round pick) showed promise (71.5 PFF grade) but only played 205 snaps. If Davis returns on a short deal, the immediate need lessens, but the Saints need a long-term replacement regardless.
needs.current_starter: Demario Davis (UFA, 37 years old — mutual interest in return), Pete Werner (under contract)
needs.draft_range_target: Round 3-5 (unless Davis doesn't return, then Round 2)

needs.explanation: No OT signing was made in early free agency. With a young QB in Tyler Shough behind a line that still has questions at tackle, OT remains a legitimate need. The Edwards signing helps run blocking by improving the interior, but the tackle spots are unchanged. Still a rounds 2-3 target.

Bryan Bresee is a good building block as a pass-rushing DT (2.5 sacks in 2025, though underwhelming overall) and the Saints will likely pick up his 5th-year option. However, the nose tackle position needs an upgrade — Davon Godchaux is replacement-level and a cut candidate. Nathan Shepherd is serviceable depth. Vernon Broughton (2025 3rd-rounder) missed most of the season with injury. Adding a run-stuffing nose tackle would unlock the pass rush for Bresee and Young.
needs.current_starter: Bryan Bresee (3-tech, under contract), Davon Godchaux (NT, potential cut candidate), Nathan Shepherd (depth)
needs.depth: Vernon Broughton (2025 3rd-round pick, injured in 2025), Jonathan Bullard (UFA), John Ridgeway (UFA)

needs.explanation: Juwan Johnson (re-signed last offseason) returns as TE1, Noah Fant added on a 2-year depth deal. But Taysom Hill (likely retired) and Foster Moreau (voided) both left. Fant partially fills the gap but isn't a true starter. TE remains a tier 3 mid-round draft target (rounds 3-5).

The interior offensive line was a disaster in 2025. Right guard Cesar Ruiz was described as 'abysmal, a human turnstile.' Left guard was a revolving door — Trevor Penning was traded mid-season for being ineffective, and replacement Dillon Radunz (UFA) was penalty-prone. Center Erik McCoy missed 10 games (torn biceps) for the second straight year. Luke Fortner (UFA) filled in well but is a free agent. The tackles (Banks, Fuaga) are excellent, but the interior needs a complete overhaul. ESPN reports the Saints seem more likely to target OL in free agency than WR.
needs.current_starter: LG: VACANT (Radunz UFA, Penning traded), C: Erik McCoy (injury prone, missed 10+ games in consecutive years), RG: Cesar Ruiz (poor performance)
needs.depth: Torricelli Simpkins III (depth), Luke Fortner (UFA, may or may not return)
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-4 (likely address one guard in FA, draft another)

needs.explanation: Travis Etienne signing (4yr/$52M) fully addresses the RB need. He is a proven 1,000-yard rusher with receiving chops who fills the exact production gap the Saints had. Alvin Kamara remains on the roster (possible trade candidate) with Kendre Miller and Devin Neal as depth. RB is no longer a draft priority.

Justin Reid was signed in 2025 and finished strong (6 PBUs, 1 INT returned for TD). Rookie Jonas Sanker (2025 3rd-rounder) showed excellent range and instincts. Julian Blackmon is a UFA. The safety position is relatively set if Reid and Sanker are the starters, but losing Blackmon reduces depth. Not a premium need.
needs.current_starter: Justin Reid (FS, under contract), Jonas Sanker (SS, under contract)
needs.depth: Julian Blackmon (UFA), Ugo Amadi (UFA), Terrell Burgess (UFA) — depth thins significantly

free_agency_impact: Cameron Jordan (EDGE) → TBD/unsigned (free agent as of 3/11)
Signed Travis Etienne Jr. (RB) from Jacksonville Jaguars — $13.0M AAV
Signed David Edwards (IOL) from Buffalo Bills — $15.2M AAV
Signed Noah Fant (TE) from Cincinnati Bengals — $4.4M AAV
Signed Kaden Elliss (LB) from Atlanta Falcons — $11.0M AAV
Signed Ryan Wright (P) from Minnesota Vikings — $3.5M AAV
Signed John Ridgeway (IDL) from Dallas Cowboys — $3.1M AAV
scheme_fit_notes: HC Kellen Moore runs a creative, play-action heavy offense from his Eagles OC days — values versatile skill players who can create YAC and explosive plays. The Saints' need for a WR2 or dynamic RB who can catch passes is amplified in Moore's system. DC Brandon Staley runs a 4-3/multiple scheme that asks EDGE rushers to play both standup and hand-down. A true nose tackle who can absorb double teams would unlock Bresee and Young as interior/edge penetrators. Staley's defense also uses the nickel/STAR position heavily, making Taylor's potential departure especially impactful — they need a versatile slot CB who can blitz.
